finding: Plan covers all 7 Electron APIs (getPath, setPath, isPackaged, getName, getVersion, getAppId, setName) consumed across the 11 files. The `PlatformPaths` interface is narrowly scoped to path and identity resolution — lifecycle (app.on, app.relaunch, app.exit) and Chromium-specific features (app.commandLine) are correctly excluded.

The getDefaultPlatform() singleton pattern creates implicit coupling — all consumers share the same platform instance. This is acceptable because in the Electron build there's only one platform (Electron), and for browser targets the entry point would provide its own. However, there's a subtle initialization ordering concern: getDefaultPlatform() could be called at module load time before app.whenReady. The current code already has this same problem (electron-store in store.ts comments about it). Mitigation: ensure ElectronPlatformPaths lazy-initializes or that the singleton only does so after app.whenReady starts.

When adding an optional paths parameter to existing functions, existing callers that don't pass it will use the default platform. This preserves backward compatibility but means the DI is optional (not enforced). For a more rigorous approach, consider making the paths parameter required in a follow-up after all callers are migrated. For now, the optional-with-default approach minimizes risk and is recommended.

The pattern of using optional parameters with a getDefaultPlatform() fallback is consistent with the codebase's existing pattern (e.g., getStore() uses lazy initialization). No convention violation found. The Plan correctly preserves all dirty/untracked files (ipc-channels.ts, app-config.ts) by using additive-only changes.
